= rpk registry context
:description: Manage schema registry contexts. Schema contexts provide namespace isolation within the schema registry, allowing multiple independent sets of subjects and schemas to coexist.
:page-platforms: linux,darwin

// This content is autogenerated. Do not edit manually. To customize content, see the writer's guide: https://github.com/redpanda-data/docs-extensions-and-macros/blob/main/docs-data/RPK_OVERRIDES_GUIDE.adoc

// tag::single-source[]
Manage schema registry contexts.

Schema contexts provide namespace isolation within the schema registry,
allowing multiple independent sets of subjects and schemas to coexist.

Before using schema contexts, the cluster must have the
schema_registry_enable_qualified_subjects configuration set to true. You
can enable it with:

[,bash]
----
rpk cluster config set schema_registry_enable_qualified_subjects true
----

Use the `--schema-context` flag on the parent `registry` command to scope
operations to a specific context.

== Usage

[,bash]
----
rpk registry context [flags]
----




== Subcommands

[cols="1,2a"]
|===
|Command |Description

|xref:reference:rpk/rpk-registry/rpk-registry-context-delete.adoc[`rpk registry context delete`]
|Delete a schema registry context. A context can only be deleted once all subjects within it have been hard deleted.

|xref:reference:rpk/rpk-registry/rpk-registry-context-list.adoc[`rpk registry context list`]
|List schema registry contexts.

|===


== Global flags

[cols="1m,1a,2a"]
|===
|Value |Type |Description

|--config |string |Redpanda or `rpk` config file; default search paths are `/Users/jakecahill/Library/Application Support/rpk/rpk.yaml`, `$PWD/redpanda.yaml`, and `/etc/redpanda/redpanda.yaml`.
|-X, --config-opt |stringArray |Override `rpk` configuration settings; `-X help` for detail or `-X list` for terser detail.
|--ignore-profile |bool |Ignore `rpk.yaml` and `redpanda.yaml`; use default settings.
|--profile |string |`rpk` profile to use.
|-v, --verbose |bool |Enable verbose logging.
|===


== Suggested reading

* xref:reference:properties/cluster-properties.adoc#schema_registry_enable_qualified_subjects[`schema_registry_enable_qualified_subjects`]
